para.bold: Pointer to the data for the time step (t0).
Size of the Matrix form; size1*size2.
Pointer to the lesser part for the time step (t0). 'data_+ $(tc+1+tc)$ * element_size' correponds to (0,0)-component of $ G^<(t0,t0-tc)$ and 'les_(tc)'.
Pointer to the retarded part for the time step (t0). 'data_+ $tc$ * element_size' correponds to (0,0)-component of $ G^<(t0,t0-tc)$ and 'ret_(tc)'.
Number of the colums in the Matrix form.
Number of the rows in the Matrix form.

briefdescription.para: Clears the stored timestep data (set to zero).
Returns the time elemet size = size1*size2.
Pointer to lesser component at a given time, representing G^<(t0,t0-deltj).
Reads data from an HDF5 file with a specified filename and group name.
Reads data from an HDF5 file for the specified group ID.
Pointer to retarded component at a given time, representing G^R(t0,t0-deltj).

memberdef.argsstring: (int i1, int i2, herm_matrix_moving< T > &G, int delti_g, int j1, int j2)
memberdef.name: set_matrixelement

memberdef.argsstring: (int i1, int i2, herm_matrix_timestep_moving< T > &G, int j1, int j2)
memberdef.name: set_matrixelement

memberdef.argsstring: (int i1, int i2, herm_matrix_timestep_moving_view< T > &G, int j1, int j2)
memberdef.name: set_matrixelement

briefdescription.para: Clears the stored timestep data. (set to zero).
Returns the number of rows (CurrentlyL always the same as number of columns!).
Scales the stored values by a scalar.
Writes data to an HDF5 file with a specified filename and group name.
Writes data to an HDF5 file for the specified group ID.
Stored tha data at one memory-truncated timeslice of a greens function.

detaileddescription.para: The herm_matrix_timestep_moving represent a Greens function at physical timestep t0. Arguments of the moving timestep are understood relative to t0: this->retptr(trelj) points to G^ret(t0,t0-trelj), this->lesptr(trelj) points to G^les(t0,t0-trelj) for trelj=0,...,tc_ the pysical timestep t0 is not stored

Each element of the Greens function is a size1 x size2 matrix. Currently only size1 = size2 is supported
T
Data type (typically a numerical type like double or complex<double>).
